RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant/Cross-Complainant Critchfield Mechanical Inc.'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Cross-Complaint.(Per Ex Parte Order Filed 1/25/19).

Defendant and Cross-Complainant Critchfield Mechanical, Inc.'s motion for leave to file a first amended cross-complaint is granted. California's policy of liberality in amendment and the interest in judicial economy warrant granting the motion. Plaintiff CDC San Francisco LLC has not shown that it will be prejudiced by allowing the amendment.

Off Calendar Jun-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Bryan Gragg, Esq. 213-395-7620 of Hanson Bridgett for Plaintiff CDC SAN FRANCISCO.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CDC SAN FRANCISCO LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANT CTRITCHFIELD MECHANICAL, INC.'S CR0SS-COMPLAINT.

Plaintiff CDC San Francisco LLC's motion to strike the entirety of the cross-complaint filed by defendant Critchfield Mechanical, Inc., is granted in its entirety without prejudice to Critchfield seeking leave to file a cross-complaint that seeks relief against at least one non-fictiously named cross-defendant. No useful purpose is achieved by an indemnity cross-complaint against only fictiously named cross-defendants, Critchfield suffers no prejudice by the granting of this motion since the statute of limitations does not begin to run on any indemnity claim it may have until after liability has been found against it, and the pendency of the cross-complaint could delay this case to the prejudice of CDC San Francisco.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
